description: Rivers, lakes, seas, and frozen waters. Places where currents pull, depths hide old secrets, and the surface never tells the whole story.
locations: Whirlpool
The water spirals with a low, hungry roar. Debris circles the rim before vanishing into a throat of black water that swallows everything whole.
Vent Field
Columns of shimmering heat twist upward from cracks in the ocean floor. The water here tastes of iron and the stone is warm to the touch.
Vent Plumes
Dark billowing clouds rise from the seabed, staining the water black and hot. Strange shapes drift at the edges, drawn to the warmth.
Volcano Cone
A mountain rises from the deep, its peak crowned in steam and fissures of molten glow. The water near its slopes is bath-warm and stinks of sulfur.
Sunken Vessel
A hull rests on its side in the murk, rigging trailing like dead vines. Fish dart through the shattered deck where something still glints below.
Sunken Fleet
Masts jut from the silt like a drowned forest. Dozens of ships lie tangled together, their holds sealed and their stories lost to the current.
Sunken Treasure
Gold coins carpet the sand between broken timbers. The glint is almost too easy to spot, as though something wants visitors to reach down.
Sunken City
Rooftops and broken towers emerge from the green murk. Streets of pale stone vanish into deeper water where faint lights still flicker.
Sunken Temple
Carved pillars rise from the silt, still holding a roof crusted with barnacles. The altar inside is clean, as though something still tends it.
Siren Shoals
Beautiful singing drifts across the water, faint and warm. The rocks just beneath the surface are littered with the splintered bones of ships.
Ship Graveyard
Broken hulls crowd the shallows, heaped and rotting. The tide groans through their ribs. Gulls circle but never land.
Seamount
The ocean floor rises sharply here into a flat-topped peak. Currents swirl with unusual force, and the water above teems with restless life.
Sea Cave
Waves boom inside the dark mouth of stone. At low tide you can walk in. At high tide, whatever entered has nowhere to go.
Coral Reef
Colors riot beneath the surface, alive and sharp-edged. The reef hums with movement and clicks with hidden claws. Touch the wrong branch and it bites back.
Kelp Forest
Tall green fronds sway in the current like slow curtains. Light filters through in shifting columns. Shadows move between the stalks that could be anything.
Ford
The river widens and goes shallow over smooth stones. Easy crossing, but the banks on both sides are thick with cover where anything could wait.
Grotto
Water drips in steady echoes inside a chamber of smooth wet stone. Light enters from below, casting rippling blue across the ceiling.
Spring
Clear water wells up from bare rock, cold enough to ache. The ground around it is impossibly green, and animals have worn deep paths to reach it.
Lake
Still water stretches to the far shore, glassy and dark. The silence is total. Whatever lives below the surface has not broken it in a long time.
River
The current runs steady and brown, carrying branches and secrets from somewhere upstream. The far bank looks easy to reach but the middle runs fast and deep.
Pond
A quiet circle of water edged in reeds and cattails. Frogs go silent as you approach. The bottom is soft mud that does not let go easily.
Stream
Cold water babbles over clean stones, barely knee-deep. It seems harmless, but it comes from somewhere higher and colder, carrying a faint mineral smell.
Creek
A thin ribbon of water winds through the underbrush, almost hidden. Follow it upstream and it gets colder. Follow it down and it disappears into the earth.
Cove
Cliffs curve around a pocket of calm water, sheltered from the open sea. The sand is untouched. A perfect hiding place, which means someone else may know it too.
Crater Lake
Water fills a vast stone bowl high in the mountains. The surface is unnaturally still and deeply blue. No streams feed it and nothing seems to live in it.
Glacier Lake
Milky blue water sits at the foot of a wall of ancient ice. The cold radiates from it in waves. Chunks of ice drift and turn slowly in the stillness.
Bay
The coastline curves inward, sheltering the water from open waves. Ships anchor here, and the beach is scarred with old fire pits and drag marks.
Waterfall
Water roars over a cliff edge in a white curtain, pounding the rocks below into mist. The noise swallows every other sound. A cave mouth hides behind the veil.
Rapids
The river narrows and drops through a gauntlet of boulders. White water crashes and spits. Anything that falls in here does not choose where it goes.
Delta
The river breaks into a hundred slow fingers, spreading across flat land choked with reeds. Channels shift with every flood. Maps of this place are never right twice.
Estuary
Salt and fresh water mingle in a wide, brackish stretch. The banks are soft and treacherous. Herons stand motionless, watching the murk for movement below.
Lagoon
Warm shallow water glows turquoise over white sand. The beauty is disarming. Beneath the surface, the sand shifts in patterns that suggest something burrowing.
Fjord
Sheer cliff walls plunge straight into black water on both sides. The passage is narrow and the echoes carry for miles. Waterfalls thread down the stone.
Strait
Two landmasses squeeze the ocean into a rushing corridor. The current is brutal and the wind funnels through. Ships that lose power here do not get second chances.
Channel
A deep cut of navigable water runs between shallows. The passage is well-known but narrow. What sits on either side controls who passes through.
Tidal Pool
A shallow basin of trapped seawater, alive with small bright creatures. Anemones pulse and crabs freeze mid-step. The ocean will reclaim this in hours.
Hot Spring
Steam curls off water that glows faintly in the dimness. The heat soaks into aching bones. The mineral smell is sharp, and the rocks are stained white and orange.
Geyser
The ground rumbles and hisses before a column of boiling water erupts skyward. The spray scalds. The ground around it is fragile crust over scalding hollows.
Marsh
Tall grasses stretch in every direction over waterlogged ground. Each step squelches and sinks. The air is thick with insects and the sweet rot of standing water.
Swamp
Dark water sits stagnant beneath a canopy of twisted trees draped in moss. The air is heavy and warm. Bubbles rise from the mud without explanation.
Bog
Spongy ground gives way underfoot to black water that smells of peat and decay. The mist sits low and thick. Old things are preserved here, not all of them natural.
Wetland
Water and land blur together across a flat expanse of reeds and shallow pools. Birds rise in startled clouds. The footing is never certain and the paths shift with the season.
Mudflat
The tide has pulled back to reveal a vast stretch of gleaming mud, pocked with worm holes and crab burrows. The stink is powerful. Things lost at sea wash up here.
Archipelago
Islands dot the water in a scattered chain, each one different. Some are green and lush, others bare rock. The channels between them run with unpredictable currents.
Inlet
A narrow finger of sea reaches inland between wooded banks. The water is deep and quiet. Small boats could hide here unseen from the open coast.
Sound
A broad protected waterway separates the mainland from a long island. The water is calmer than the open sea but deep enough to hold old wrecks and older things.
Gulf
The ocean pushes far inland, creating a vast curved basin of deep water. The shores are distant and hazy. Storms that enter here have nowhere to spend their fury but the coast.
Confluence
Two rivers collide in a churning seam of different-colored water. The current is confused and powerful. Debris collects in spinning eddies at the joining.
Oxbow Lake
A crescent of still water sits apart from the river that abandoned it. Lily pads cover the surface. It feels forgotten, a quiet pocket the current left behind.
Plunge Pool
A deep basin carved by falling water, churning white at the center and clear at the edges. The mist keeps everything damp. Smooth stones ring the bottom like teeth.
Cascade
Water spills down a staircase of stone ledges, each drop feeding the next in a chain of white curtains. The roar builds as you descend. The rocks are slick and unforgiving.
Cenote
A round hole in the limestone opens to reveal blue water far below. Vines trail down the walls. The ancient stains on the rock ledge suggest this place once had a purpose.
Sinkhole
The ground collapsed here, revealing a deep pit half-filled with dark water. The edges are crumbling and unstable. Something pale moves in the depths.
Artesian Spring
Water pushes up through the rock with steady pressure, welling into a clear pool without any visible source. The ground around it stays lush even in drought.
Thermal Spring
Warm water seeps from the rock, filling a series of shallow pools that steam in the cool air. The minerals have painted the stone in streaks of rust and gold.
Mineral Spring
The water here tastes of salt and copper and leaves a white crust on every stone it touches. People once traveled far to drink it. The old shelters still stand.
Underground River
Black water rushes through a stone passage, the current fast and echoing. The ceiling is low and the banks are narrow. Wherever it goes, no light has ever followed.
Underground Lake
A vast still surface stretches into darkness beyond the reach of any torch. The silence is absolute. A dropped stone echoes for a long, long time.
Flooded Cavern
Water fills a cave system to the ceiling in places, leaving only inches of air in others. The walls are slick and the current subtle but insistent.
Tide Cave
At low tide the cave is walkable, its walls crusted with salt and shell. At high tide the ocean fills it entirely. Timing is everything here.
Blowhole
A crack in the coastal rock erupts with a geyser of seawater every time a wave hits below. The spray is violent and the ground around it trembles with each blast.
Natural Arch
The sea has eaten through a headland, leaving a bridge of stone over crashing water. The span is narrow and the wind tears through. Walking across is a dare.
Beaver Pond
A wall of sticks and mud has backed the stream into a wide, shallow lake. The water is still and dark with tannin. Gnawed stumps ring the shore like old sentries.
Frozen Lake
Ice stretches from shore to shore, thick enough to walk on but groaning with every step. Snow covers the surface. Beneath the ice, dark shapes drift slowly.
Frozen River
The river has stopped, locked under a sheet of clouded ice. The banks are crusted with frost. In the silence you can hear the water still moving somewhere below.
Ice Dam
A wall of packed ice blocks the river, forcing the water to pool and spread. The pressure builds behind it with soft cracking sounds. When it breaks, everything downstream will know.
Glacial Pool
Meltwater collects in a depression of scored stone, painfully blue and cold enough to stop your heart. The glacier towers above, ancient and indifferent.
Meltwater Stream
A thin rush of icy water spills from the edge of a glacier, cutting a channel through gravel and silt. The water is cloudy white and so cold it burns.
Alpine Lake
A jewel of blue water sits in a high stone bowl above the treeline. The air is thin and sharp. Wind ripples the surface under a sky that feels close enough to touch.
Mountain Lake
Evergreens crowd the shore of a cold, clear lake nestled between peaks. The reflections are perfect and undisturbed. Sound carries strangely across the water here.
Salt Lake
White crusted shores surround water so dense nothing sinks. The air shimmers with heat and the smell of brine. Nothing grows at the waterline. Nothing drinks here.
Brine Pool
A pocket of water denser than the sea around it sits on the ocean floor like a lake within a lake. Its edges shimmer. Creatures that wander in do not wander out.
Ship
Timbers creak and sails snap overhead. The deck tilts with each swell, and the horizon stretches in every direction with nothing to hold onto.
Harbor
Masts crowd the waterline and ropes creak against pilings. The air smells of salt and tar, and cargo moves in steady rhythm.
